rulings.Case Title: PAUL FRENKIEL ET AL VS. JO ELLEN GREEN KAISER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Stipulated Motion And Motion To Sever
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for April 2, 2025, line 10. DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY, MERCURY INSURANCE COMPANY's Stipulated Motion To Sever. Defendants' Stipulated Motion to Sever is DENIED. Several items in plaintiffs' prayer for relief are asserted against both the insurance defendants and the individual defendants. Supplemental declaration filed by the plaintiffs' counsel is unclear as to whether severing the action would result in plaintiffs seeking duplicative damages in two separate actions. Unfiled supplemental briefings were not considered. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/HLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC VS. FAY SERVICING LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for April 2, 2025, line 2. DEFENDANT FAY SERVICING LLC, U.S. BANK TRUST NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS OWNER, WRIGHT, FINLAY & ZAK, LLP's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT Defendant's Demurrer to the First Amended Complaint is OVERRULED, in part and SUSTAINED, in part with leave to amend. The First Cause of Action for Specific Performance is sustained with leave to amend, to be pled as a remedy and not as a standalone cause of action. Plaintiff must allege facts giving rise to the remedy of specific performance, the inadequacy of a legal remedy, and specifically what term in the agreement it seeks enforced, in light that the property at issue is already sold. Defendants' demurrer on the ground of standing is overruled. Defendants failed to address 11 U.S. Code 349(b)(1)(3) on reply. Defendants' cited authority only address cases where a bankruptcy case has been closed, rather than dismissed. Defendants' demurrer to the Second Cause of Action for Wrongful Foreclosure is overruled. Plaintiff's claim for wrongful foreclosure is premised on Defendants' violation of the agreement to accept monthly payment and not foreclose. (FAC 24-25.) With respect to tender, defendants' cited authority suggests tender is required when there is an alleged irregularity in the notice or procedure of the sale, not when there is a challenge to the trustee's right to conduct the sale at all. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/HLJ)
